question.text: Regulation 4(2)(a) of SEBI LODR Regulations 2015 obliges listed entities to protect shareholder rights. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as such a right?

answer.text: Right to participate in decisions concerning fundamental corporate changes
Opportunity to participate effectively and vote in general shareholder meetings
Right to appoint or remove the proxy advisor of the company
Exercise of ownership rights by all shareholders, including institutional investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The rights mentioned under Regulation 4(2)(a) include participation in corporate changes, voting in meetings, participation in governance decisions like board nominations, and exercise of ownership rights. Appointing/removing proxy advisors is not a shareholder right under this regulation. **The Trap:** Since proxy advisors are discussed in the same context, one might confuse proxy advisor appointment as a shareholder right. **Cross-Exam Context:** LODR obligations are extensively tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Proxy advisors serve shareholders but are not appointed by them through corporate governance processes.
